BRA|NOR.text: Brazil face Norway in the World Cup last 16, with the model favouring Brazil to advance. Norway are appearing at their first World Cup in 28 years and are seeking a maiden quarter-final berth. The tie also features a meeting involving Erling Haaland as Norway take on Brazil.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
Stay humble - the Premier League feud about to hit world stage
Brazil going out? Chris Sutton predicts World Cup's last-16 matches

ENG|MEX.text: England, given a strong advance edge, face hosts Mexico in the World Cup round of 16, with the match played at altitude in Mexico City. Mexico manager Javier Aguirre acknowledged his side will need an almost perfect performance to win, while England's Thomas Tuchel expects the conditions to bring out his team's best. Captain Harry Kane has called on fans back home to stay up for the late kick-off.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
Fast-food chain Greggs benches Mexican sandwich ahead on England World Cup match
Aguirre: Mexico will need an almost perfect match to beat England

BEL|USA.text:
Belgium enter as the stronger side per the model (72% to advance). 

Note: The provided source refers to Senegal's World Cup exit and internal disputes, not this fixture, so no relevant team news is available for USA v Belgium.

sources.headline: Senegal's Ismaila Sarr refutes rumours of Pape Gueye 'altercation'; urges 'unity' after World Cup exit
